target: WIN-057 numbering collision — add WIN-057b for SAA Global Cavity Re-Pressurization claim
rationale: The dome site reuses WIN-057 for two distinct claims: (1) Two-Zone Disc Topology (our current coverage, prospective list) and (2) Global Cavity Re-Pressurization / SAA Dual-Lobe Synchronous Decay (confirmed list). These are scientifically unrelated claims sharing a number — one is about distance-mapping geometry, the other about geomagnetic anomaly interpretation. Following the 058/058b precedent, we should add WIN-057b to cover the uncovered claim. The numbering collision itself is noted in the new entry as evidence of the dome site numbering instability we critique elsewhere.

recommended_win_057b.finding: Multi-station SAA decay synchrony is expected from MHD core-flow models; the dome relabels a well-documented phenomenon as "cavity re-pressurization" without any mechanism. Substantially duplicates WIN-004/005/041 evidence.
recommended_win_057b.detail_claim: The dome claims that TTB (Namibia), VSS (Brazil), and HER (South Africa) INTERMAGNET stations show synchronous magnetic decay acceleration, proving a "global Ovoid Shoulder shift" — a physical rupture in the dome's Bottom Plate — rather than regional fluid-core flow. The dome site lists this as WIN-057 in the confirmed predictions section, creating a numbering collision with WIN-057 (Two-Zone Disc Topology) in the prospective predictions list. The claim asserts two SAA lobes: Lobe A (South American, 26.6°S, 49.1°W, receding westward) and Lobe B (African, 20.0°S, 10.0°E, accelerating), with the conclusion that this is "not a local fluid shift" but evidence of a cavity-scale structural change at r ≈ 20,000 km.
recommended_win_057b.detail_evidence:
Kernel of truth: The SAA really has bifurcated into two distinct intensity minima, and INTERMAGNET stations across Southern Africa and South America really do show correlated secular variation trends. These are genuine observations from a real geophysical monitoring network. The dome author correctly identifies the dual-lobe structure and its spatial coordinates.

Why it fails: Standard magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) models explain both the SAA splitting and the multi-station correlation from first principles. Terra-Nova et al. (2017) demonstrate that reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (CMB) drive the SAA's spatial structure, drift, and bifurcation. The "synchronous" decay across TTB, VSS, and HER is expected because all three stations sit within the SAA's spatial correlation length of approximately 5,000–8,000 km — they are sampling the same large-scale geomagnetic anomaly, not three independent phenomena. The correlated secular variation is a direct consequence of large-scale CMB flow patterns, which MHD simulations reproduce without invoking cavity ruptures. Alken et al. (2020) further document the SAA's second minimum emerging near 20°S, 10°E (matching the dome's "Lobe B") as a natural consequence of reverse-flux patch migration — exactly where MHD predicts it.

The dome has no mechanism. "Global Ovoid Shoulder shift at r ≈ 20,000 km" is a label, not a derivation. The dome model contains no equations of fluid dynamics, no description of what physical process causes the "cavity" to rupture, no prediction of when or where new lobes should form, and no explanation for the asymmetry between the two lobes (the African cell decays faster despite similar distances from the pole). Standard MHD explains the asymmetry via the African large-low-shear-velocity province altering local core flow — the dome's radially symmetric aetheric framework cannot.

Massive overlap with existing WINs. This claim draws on the same INTERMAGNET station data, the same SAA splitting phenomenon, and the same secular variation trends already counted in WIN-004 (SAA exponential separation), WIN-005 (African SAA cell faster decay), WIN-041 (SAA multi-station decay), and WIN-035 (SAA African threshold). Counting the same geophysical process under a fifth label inflates the win count without introducing new evidence. See our discussion in WIN-041 for the thermometer analogy: counting each station measuring the same room's temperature as a separate confirmation.

Note on dome-site numbering: The confirmed predictions section lists WIN-057 as "Global Cavity Re-Pressurization" while the prospective predictions list assigns WIN-057 to "Two-Zone Disc Topology" (see WIN-057). These are scientifically unrelated claims sharing a number — one concerns distance-mapping geometry, the other SAA interpretation — illustrating the numbering instability we document in Part 2b.
recommended_win_057b.detail_verdict_text:
The multi-station SAA decay synchrony the dome claims as "Global Cavity Re-Pressurization" is a well-documented consequence of large-scale core-mantle boundary flow. MHD simulations reproduce the SAA's dual-lobe structure, station-to-station correlation, and asymmetric decay rates from first principles. The dome relabels this standard physics as "Ovoid Shoulder shift" without deriving any quantitative prediction from its geometry. Furthermore, the underlying observations substantially duplicate evidence already counted in WIN-004, WIN-005, WIN-035, and WIN-041 — this is the same anomaly, measured by the same network, interpreted through the same data.

Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. The confirming values come from publicly available INTERMAGNET station archives. We could not locate a pre-registration of the specific synchronous-decay prediction in a timestamping venue independent of the author. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is retrodiction, not prediction.
recommended_win_057b.tldr_evidence: Three INTERMAGNET stations (TTB, VSS, HER) show correlated magnetic decay — but they all sit within the SAA's 5,000–8,000 km correlation length, so synchrony is expected. MHD simulations reproduce the SAA dual-lobe structure and its drift from core-mantle boundary flow, without invoking cavity ruptures.
recommended_win_057b.tldr_verdict: Standard MHD explains the synchronous SAA decay from core dynamics. The dome relabels this as "cavity re-pressurization" with no derivation and substantially duplicates evidence from WIN-004, 005, 035, and 041.

kernel_of_truth: The SAA really has bifurcated into two intensity minima, and the multi-station decay trends are genuinely correlated. The dome author correctly identifies the dual-lobe coordinates and the cross-station correlation pattern. These are real geophysical observations from a reputable monitoring network.
holes_addressed: Coverage gap: dome site WIN-057 (confirmed) was not evaluated by our review
Numbering collision between prospective WIN-057 and confirmed WIN-057 documented
new_evidence_added: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) — MHD derivation of SAA splitting from CMB reversed-flux patches
Alken et al. (2020) — second SAA minimum emergence matching dome Lobe B coordinates
SAA spatial correlation length 5,000–8,000 km explains multi-station synchrony
Cross-reference to WIN-004/005/035/041 for evidence duplication

anticipated_objections.objection: MHD models do not predict the EXACT timing of the dual-lobe emergence
anticipated_objections.response: True — MHD models predict the mechanism and spatial pattern, not precise timing. But neither does the dome: it provides no equation for when lobes should form. The dome merely observes the split after it happens, same as everyone else. Predicting the mechanism is stronger than predicting nothing.

anticipated_objections.objection: The synchronous acceleration is the key — individual stations were already counted, but the synchrony pattern was not
anticipated_objections.response: Synchrony within a spatial correlation length is the null hypothesis, not a discovery. Three stations within 8,000 km measuring the same large-scale anomaly SHOULD show correlated trends. The burden is on the dome to show synchrony that EXCEEDS what MHD flow patterns predict, which it does not attempt.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome is predicting a specific physical interpretation (cavity rupture), not just the observation
anticipated_objections.response: An interpretation that makes no quantitative predictions is unfalsifiable. "Cavity rupture" predicts nothing about where the next lobe forms, what the decay rate should be, or why the African lobe accelerates faster. It is a narrative overlay on standard geophysics.

numbering_note: This follows the 058/058b pattern established when the dome site assigned WIN-058 to both theta=-lonE (our WIN-058) and the Bedrock Induction Ratio kappa=1.677 (our WIN-058b). The numbering collision is itself evidence of the dome site numbering instability documented in Part 2b.
